items.title: The Galaxy Card Is Samsung’s Answer to the Apple Card
items.summary: Directly added to your Samsung Wallet account, it’s yet another cash-back credit card, this time tailored for Samsung stans.

items.title: The FDA Says It Didn't Apologize to Supplier Linked to Diarrhea Outbreak, Actually
items.summary: The agency stressed that the July 17 voluntary recall from Taylor Farms still stands. The supplier has been linked to the ongoing cyclospora outbreak.

items.title: Fans Flocked to World Cup Host Cities—Causing 80 Percent of the Tournament’s Emissions
items.summary: The latest estimates reveal that the majority of greenhouse gas emissions from large-scale events such as the FIFA World Cup stem from spectators traveling to host cities.

items.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

items.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

items.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
items.summary: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
items.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.summary: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.summary: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

items.title: Capital One bet big on Discover. Now it must prove the gamble was worth it
items.summary: Tuesday evening's second-quarter earnings report is the perfect stage to do just that.

items.title: Coinbase trading results may fall short of expectations, according to prediction markets
items.summary: Speculators think in the company's second quarter earnings report, it will show trading volume fell for a third consecutive quarter.

items.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%
items.summary: Consumer demand for cars in China tumbles following record-high sales of 23.7 million units in 2025.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Mullin: Feds ‘actively pursuing’ threat to World Cup fan fest
items.summary: Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Markwayne Mullin on Monday said federal authorities are “actively pursuing” an investigation into a threat at a World Cup fan fest event. “It was an intimate threat that because of our federal partnership and our local partnership, being able to work together, we were able to stop it before…

items.title: Rising US death count in Iran war drives Trump toward escalation
items.summary: Fears are growing that the deaths of three U.S. servicemembers over a 24-hour period this weekend could push President Trump toward further escalation, even as both sides leave diplomatic doors open.  The servicemembers’ deaths bring the U.S. death toll in the Iran war to 17 since the conflict broke out in March. Since the U.S.…

items.title: Ossoff slams RFK Jr. for terminating FoodNet, drawing link to cyclospora outbreak
items.summary: Sen. Jon Ossoff (D-Ga.) slammed Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. for ending the Center for Disease Control and Prevention’s (CDC) tracking of parasitic infections including cyclospora.  The decision was made last year and suspended tracking managed by the CDC, U.S. Department of Agriculture and the Food and Drug Administration, in addition to…

items.title: A financial planner explains why splitting your partner's $2,100 mortgage will leave you with nothing. And it's okay

items.title: Some dinosaurs could rise like giants until they grew too big
items.summary: Two elephant-sized South American sauropods were unusually well built for standing on their hind legs. Digital tests showed that their robust femurs handled the enormous forces better than those of larger species, especially while the dinosaurs were young. The upright pose may have helped them reach treetops, frighten predators, or impress potential mates.

items.title: The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation
items.summary: The mysterious sound known as The Hum has been reported worldwide for decades, yet its source often remains impossible to find. Researchers tested whether people who hear it have exceptional low-frequency hearing or measurable sounds produced inside their ears, but neither explanation fit most participants. The strongest possibility is that many cases involve low-frequency tinnitus, even though the sound seems completely external.

items.title: An ordinary laptop solved a problem thought to require a quantum computer
items.summary: A quantum problem once described as impossible for classical computers has now been solved using relatively modest hardware. Researchers used tensor networks to compress the overwhelming wave function created by hundreds of entangled qubits, allowing some calculations to run on a laptop. Their results matched both theoretical predictions and simulations performed with a quantum computer. The method could open new paths for exploring quantum dynamics and materials.

items.title: Pompeii’s survivors shine in a new documentary series
items.summary: Hosted by actor Tom Hiddleston, Pompeii: Out of Time mixes facts and fiction to bring three real ancient Romans to life.

items.title: AI ‘ghosts’ can comfort mourners — even when the bots get the facts wrong
items.summary: A small study suggests emotional fit, not perfect accuracy, may make chatbot versions of dead loved ones feel real.

items.title: In painted caves, ancient human DNA clings to the walls
items.summary: Scientists recovered ancient human DNA from cave walls for the first time. Refined, the method could help link prehistoric paintings to their makers.
